← Timetable

ASSESSING

OBJECTIVES

  • Understand why and how candidates are assessed throughout the BLS course
  • Know the criteria for a good assessment: specificity, validity, reliability, feasibility, fidelity
  • Be able to carry out continuous assessment during teaching, not just in formal tests
  • Understand how assessment results feed into certification

WHAT IS ASSESSMENT?

WHY DO ASSESSMENT?

WHO NEEDS TO BE
ABLE TO ASSESS?

BLS CONTINUOUS ASSESSMENT

Press the right arrow to reveal what each term means

SPECIFICITY

Tests only the skill being assessed — nothing unrelated.

VALIDITY

Actually measures what it claims to measure.

RELIABILITY

Gives consistent results between assessors and occasions.

FEASIBILITY

Realistic to deliver given time, resources and assessor capacity.

FIDELITY

Reflects real-life clinical conditions as closely as possible.

METHODS OF ASSESSMENT

RMD ASSESSMENT

MONDAY NIGHT SESSIONS
  • Continuous assessment
  • Skills test
  • Scenario test
→
END OF COURSE EXAM
  • Certification

BLS CONTINUOUS ASSESSMENT

  • Scenario test
  • Observe during teaching
  • Candidates need to demonstrate skills during session
  • Competence

DURING AN ASSESSMENT

Exam form

AFTERWARDS

THE RESULT

SUMMARY

  • Assessment runs throughout the whole course, not just in formal tests
  • A good assessment is specific, valid, reliable, feasible and has fidelity
  • Instructors assess continuously while teaching
